title: Determining weights of points of a point cloud based on geometric features
abstract: According to one or more embodiments, operations may comprise obtaining a first point cloud. The operations also comprise performing segmentation of the first point cloud, the segmentation generating one or more clusters of points of the point cloud. The operations also comprise determining, for each respective cluster of the plurality of clusters, a respective geometric feature of a corresponding object that corresponds to the respective cluster. The operations also comprise obtaining a second point cloud. The operations also comprise assigning a plurality of weights that comprises assigning a respective weight to each respective cluster based on the respective geometric feature that corresponds to the respective cluster. The operations also comprise obtaining a second point cloud and aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud based on the plurality of weights.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: obtaining a first point cloud; performing segmentation of the first point cloud, the segmentation generating a plurality of clusters of points of the first point cloud; determining, for each respective cluster of the plurality of clusters, a respective geometric feature of a corresponding object that corresponds to the respective cluster; assigning a plurality of weights that comprises assigning a respective weight to each respective cluster based on the respective geometric feature that corresponds to the respective cluster; obtaining a second point cloud; and aligning, based on the plurality of weights, the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the first point cloud is obtained from a LIDAR scan and the second point cloud is obtained from HD map data, further comprising determining a location of a vehicle based on the aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the first point cloud is obtained from a first LIDAR scan and the second point cloud is obtained from a second LIDAR scan, further comprising generating an HD map based on the aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the first point cloud is obtained from a LIDAR scan and the second point cloud HD map data, further comprising updating an HD map based on the aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein assigning the plurality of weights includes adjusting relative numbers of points of two or more clusters with respect to each other. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein adjusting the relative number of points includes removing points from at least one of the two or more clusters. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein assigning the plurality of weights includes assigning different priorities to points of different clusters such that alignment operations favor points given higher priority over points given lower priority. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the respective geometric feature comprises: determining a measure of spread of points along a plurality of axes; and comparing the measure of spread along a first axis with a measure of spread along a second axis to determine a shape of the corresponding object that corresponds to the respective cluster. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the first axis is normal to a horizontal plane and the second axis is parallel to the horizontal plane, wherein determining the shape of the corresponding object that corresponds to the respective cluster comprises determining whether a ratio of the measure of spread along the first axis and the measure of spread along the second axis exceeds a threshold value. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: selecting one or more respective clusters having a ratio of the measure of spread along the first axis and the measure of spread along the second axis that exceeds a threshold value; assigning higher weights to points of one or more selected clusters; and assigning lower weights to points outside of one or more selected clusters. 11. One or more non-transitory computer readable storage media storing instructions that, in response to being executed by one or more processors, cause a system to perform operations, the operations comprising: obtaining a first point cloud; performing segmentation of the first point cloud, the segmentation generating a plurality of clusters of points of the first point cloud; determining, for each respective cluster of the plurality of clusters, a respective geometric feature of a corresponding object that corresponds to the respective cluster; assigning a plurality of weights that comprises assigning a respective weight to each respective cluster based on the respective geometric feature that corresponds to the respective cluster; 

 obtaining a second point cloud; and aligning, based on the plurality of weights, the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 12. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 11, wherein the first point cloud is obtained from a LIDAR scan and the second point cloud is obtained from HD map data, and wherein the operations further comprise determining a location of a vehicle based on the aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 13. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 11, wherein the first point cloud is obtained from a first LIDAR scan and the second point cloud is obtained from a second LIDAR scan, and wherein the operations further comprise generating an HD map based on the aligning the first point cloud with the second point cloud. 14. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 11, wherein assigning the plurality of weights includes adjusting relative numbers of points of two or more clusters with respect to each other. 16. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 15, wherein adjusting the relative number of points includes removing points from at least one of the two or more clusters. 17.
